evals.name: strategy

assertions.trap: delete test accesses private _cache, testing implementation not behavior
register test uses toMatchSnapshot() instead of specific assertions
getUser test has if/else conditional logic
'register', 'should work correctly', 'delete', 'type check' are vague names
shared static testUser fixture instead of factory/builder
type check test verifies string types already guaranteed by TypeScript
uses toMatchSnapshot instead of specific field assertions
spying on Logger.info (internal implementation) instead of only mocking external boundaries

evals.name: tdd-workflow

assertions.trap: user wrote all tests first then all implementation (horizontal slicing)
should do one test → one impl → repeat, not batch
user wants to refactor while tests are failing (RED)
should write only enough code to pass current test, not anticipate

evals.name: ui-testing

assertions.trap: uses CSS class selectors (.email-input, .submit-btn) instead of role/label selectors
uses waitForTimeout(3000) multiple times instead of proper waits
registers user through UI instead of API seeding
no POM, selectors/actions inline in test
shares browser context, previous test contaminates state
test is flaky due to shared state and arbitrary timeouts
real payment gateway (4242...) instead of mocked service

evals.name: gotchas

assertions.trap: mockFetch defined before vi.mock but vi.mock hoists — mockFetch will be undefined
manual cleanup() call in afterEach when Vitest does it automatically
uses page.click() and page.$() instead of locator pattern with auto-wait assertions
toMatchSnapshot on empty state instead of specific assertion
